messages.content: Show me a Motoi example of `ai/align`.

```motoi
(ai/align 'fish-1 'school 50)
```

Steer fish-1 toward the average heading of all entities tagged 'school within radius 50.
Give me an intermediate example of `ai/align`.

```motoi
(define (update-flock ctx)
  (for-each (lambda (id) (ai/align id 'boid 100 2.0)) (ctx-get 'boid-ids ctx))
  (next 'flock-moving ctx))
```

Apply alignment to all boid IDs in context, with a force cap of 2.0; then advance the state.
Give me an expert example of `ai/align`.

```motoi
(define (choreograph-dance ctx)
  (let ((formation (ctx-get 'formation ctx)) (radius (if (eq? formation 'tight) 30 100)))
    (for-each
      (lambda (dancer-id)
        (ai/align dancer-id 'dancer radius 1.5)
        (ai/cohere dancer-id 'dancer radius 1.0)
        (ai/separate dancer-id 'dancer 20 2.0))
      (ctx-get 'dancer-ids ctx))
    (next 'dancing ctx)))
```

Combine alignment, cohesion, and separation for coordinated group choreography; radius and force scale with formation tightness.

Show me a Motoi example of `ai/arrive`.

```motoi
(define my-entity 'my-entity)
(ai/arrive my-entity 100 200)
```

Entity steers toward point (100, 200) with default slow-radius and force.
Give me an intermediate example of `ai/arrive`.

```motoi

(let ((target-x 150) (target-y 75))
  (ai/arrive my-entity target-x target-y 30))
```

Provide explicit slow-radius (30 pixels); entity decelerates smoothly in that zone.
Give me an expert example of `ai/arrive`.

```motoi
(define (patrol-waypoint entity waypoint-list waypoint-idx)
  (let ((wp (list-ref waypoint-list waypoint-idx))
        (wx (car wp))
        (wy (cadr wp)))
    (ai/arrive entity wx wy 40 8.0)
    (if (< (entity/distance entity wx wy) 10)
        (+ waypoint-idx 1)
        waypoint-idx)))
```

Cycle through patrol points; arrive at each with custom slow-radius and force, advance when close enough.
Give me a novice-level example of `ai/arrive`.

```motoi

```

Steer the hero to (100, 100). Slows down as it gets close so it doesn't overshoot.
Give me an intermediate example of `ai/arrive`.

```motoi

  (lambda ()
    (ai/arrive 'hero 200 50 30 4)))
```

Arrive with a 30-unit slow radius and a steering force of 4. Feels smooth.
Show me a Motoi example of `ai/bb-del!`.

```motoi

```

Delete the patrol-target key from entity-42's memory.

```motoi

```

Give me an expert example of `ai/bb-del!`.

```motoi
(define (reset-ai-state entity)
  (for-each (lambda (key)
              (ai/bb-del! entity key))
            '(patrol-target alert-level combat-stance last-seen-pos)))
```

Batch-clear multiple blackboard keys; useful for resetting NPC state after a combat phase ends.

```motoi

```

Store a goal, then forget it. The delete returns #t because the key existed.

Only try to delete a key that's actually there. Defensive tidy-up.
Show me a Motoi example of `ai/bb-get`.

```motoi
(define my-entity 'my-entity)
(ai/bb-get my-entity 'alert-level)
```

Retrieve the alert-level; if not set, return 'no-key.
Give me an expert example of `ai/bb-get`.

```motoi
(define (behavior-decide entity)
  (let ((alert (ai/bb-get entity 'alert-level 0))
        (seen-pos (ai/bb-get entity 'last-seen-pos null)))
    (cond ((> alert 3) (ai/bb-set! entity 'state 'flee))
          ((not (null? seen-pos)) (ai/bb-set! entity 'state 'investigate))
          (else (ai/bb-set! entity 'state 'idle)))))
```

Decision tree reads two keys with defaults; branches logic on their values and sets next state.

If mood isn't set, fall back to 'neutral. Default protects against missing keys.

Give me an expert example of `ai/bb-has?`.

```motoi
(define (update-patrol entity waypoints idx)
  (if (ai/bb-has? entity 'patrol-active?)
      (let ((next-idx (+ idx 1)))
        (ai/bb-set! entity 'current-wp (list-ref waypoints (modulo next-idx (length waypoints))))
        next-idx)
      idx))
```

Only advance patrol if the patrol-active? flag is set; safe, non-crashing branching.

Set multiple keys when an event occurs; subsequent ticks read these to decide behavior.
Give me an expert example of `ai/bb-set!`.

```motoi
(define (combat-state entity target-entity)
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'in-combat? #t)
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'target-id (entity/id target-entity))
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'combat-start-time (frame))
  (ai/bb-set! entity 'health-at-combat-start (entity/ref target-entity 'hp))
  (ai/bt-tick combat-tree entity))
```

Initialize a multi-key combat state; use those keys as input to the combat behavior tree.

Show me a Motoi example of `ai/bt-tick`.

```motoi

(define simple-tree (ai/bt-action 'patrol))
(define status (ai/bt-tick simple-tree my-entity-id))
(display status)
```

Simplest case: a single action node. ai/bt-tick calls the registered 'patrol' action and returns its result.
Give me an intermediate example of `ai/bt-tick`.

```motoi
(define goblin-id 'goblin-id)
(define tree
  (ai/bt-sequence
    (ai/bt-condition 'target-seen)
    (ai/bt-action 'move-to-target)
    (ai/bt-action 'shoot)))

(define (on-tick)
  (let ((outcome (ai/bt-tick tree goblin-id)))
    (cond ((equal? outcome 'success) (display "Attack succeeded!"))
          ((equal? outcome 'failure) (display "Attack failed")))))

(on-tick)
```

Each call to ai/bt-tick executes the tree once: check sight, move, shoot. Return status tells you if the attack completed.
Give me an expert example of `ai/bt-tick`.

```motoi
(define all-agents '())
(define combat-tree
  (ai/bt-selector
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'see-enemy)
      (ai/bt-action 'strafe-and-shoot))
    (ai/bt-sequence
      (ai/bt-condition 'hear-enemy)
      (ai/bt-action 'move-toward-sound))
    (ai/bt-action 'patrol)))

(define (game-loop)
  (for-each (lambda (agent)
    (let ((result (ai/bt-tick combat-tree agent)))
      (when (equal? result 'running) (display "Action ongoing\n"))))
    all-agents))

(game-loop)
```

Typical game loop: tick the behavior tree once per agent per frame. The tree encodes the entire decision hierarchy (see → strafe, hear → approach, else patrol). Stateful actions (those returning 'running') are resumed each tick.

Each bird steers toward the center of all other birds within 30 units and tagged 'flock'.
Give me an intermediate example of `ai/cohere`.

```motoi
(define all-birds '())
(define (advanced-flock)
  (for-each (lambda (bird)
    (ai/separate bird 'flock 10)  ; avoid close neighbors
    (ai/align bird 'flock 20)     ; match neighbor heading
    (ai/cohere bird 'flock 30 5)) ; steer toward center, capped force
    all-birds))

(advanced-flock)
```

Three boid rules in sequence: separate (collision avoidance), align (velocity matching), cohere (cohesion). Together they create realistic flocking.

Show me a Motoi example of `ai/decide`.

```motoi
(define soldier-id 'soldier-id)
(define choices
  (list (cons 'attack (lambda (id) (if (target-nearby? id) 10 0)))
        (cons 'flee (lambda (id) (if (hurt? id) 8 0)))))

(ai/decide soldier-id choices)
```

Two choices scored by simple conditions. If target is nearby, attack scores 10; if hurt, flee scores 8. Highest scorer wins.
Give me an intermediate example of `ai/decide`.

```motoi
(define warrior-id 'warrior-id)
(define (threat-level id)
  (let ((enemy-dist (distance-to-nearest-enemy id))
        (my-health (current-health id)))
    (* (/ 100 (max enemy-dist 1)) (/ my-health 100))))

(define choices
  (list (cons 'aggressive-attack (lambda (id) (if (> (threat-level id) 5) 12 0)))
        (cons 'defensive-shoot (lambda (id) (if (> (threat-level id) 2) 7 1)))
        (cons 'retreat (lambda (id) (if (< (threat-level id) 1) 5 0)))))

(ai/decide warrior-id choices)
```

Threat level combines distance and health into a single metric. Three actions scored based on threat: high threat → aggressive, medium → defensive, low → retreat.
Give me an expert example of `ai/decide`.

```motoi
(define mercenary-id 'mercenary-id)
(define (tactical-choice id)
  (define (attack-score id)
    (let ((target (nearest-enemy id))
          (ammo (get-ammo-count id)))
      (if (and target (> ammo 0))
        (+ (* 2 (max 0 (- 50 (distance-to id target))))
           (* ammo 0.5))
        0)))
  
  (define (defend-score id)
    (let ((allies (friendly-units-nearby id 30)))
      (if (> (length allies) 0)
        (+ 5 (length allies))
        0)))
  
  (define (loot-score id)
    (let ((items (items-nearby id 20)))
      (if (> (length items) 0)
        (+ 3 (* (length items) 2))
        0)))
  
  (ai/decide id
    (list (cons 'shoot attack-score)
          (cons 'cover defend-score)
          (cons 'loot loot-score))))

(tactical-choice mercenary-id)
```

Each choice's score is a weighted combination of game state (distance, ammo, allies, loot). Decision adapts to current situation dynamically.
Give me a novice-level example of `ai/decide`.

```motoi

```

Score each option. The higher score wins. The chosen action fires.

```motoi
(display (choose 'hero 0.2))
```

Player evades nearest enemy if one exists, otherwise wanders
Give me an expert example of `ai/evade`.

```motoi
(define (update-threat ctx)
  (let* ((enemies (world/find-all world 'enemy))
         (closest (fold-left (lambda (best e)
                               (if (< (entity/distance player-id e)
                                      (entity/distance player-id best))
                                 e best))
                             (car enemies) (cdr enemies))))
    (ai/evade player-id closest (ctx-get 'evasion-force ctx))))
```

Multi-threat scenario: fold to find closest enemy, then evade with configurable force

Give me an expert example of `ai/flee`.

```motoi
(define (flee-from-centroid ctx)
  (let* ((enemies (world/find-all world 'enemy))
         (cx (/ (fold-left (lambda (sum e) (+ sum (entity/x e))) 0 enemies)
```

```motoi
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc init))
    (if (null? xs) acc (loop (cdr xs) (f acc (car xs))))))
(define (count-live-neighbors col row)
  (let* ((deltas (list -1 0 1))
         (candidates (fold-left (lambda (acc dx)
                   (fold-left (lambda (a2 dy)
                     (if (and (not (and (= dx 0) (= dy 0)))
                              (grid-cell? (+ col dx) (+ row dy)))
                       (+ a2 1) a2))
                     acc deltas))
                   0 deltas)))
    candidates))
```

Give me an intermediate example of `random-int`.

```motoi
(define (take xs n)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (n n) (acc '()))
    (if (or (zero? n) (null? xs))
        (reverse acc)
        (loop (cdr xs) (- n 1) (cons (car xs) acc)))))
(define (drop xs n)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (n n))
    (if (or (zero? n) (null? xs)) xs (loop (cdr xs) (- n 1)))))
(define (shuffle lst)
  (if (null? lst) lst
      (let* ((i (random-int (length lst)))
             (x (list-ref lst i))
             (rest (append (take lst i) (drop lst (+ i 1)))))
        (cons x (shuffle rest)))))
(shuffle '(a b c d))
```

Give me an expert example of `viewport`.

```motoi
(define (fit-card-in-view card-id)
  (let* ((rect (card-screen-rect card-id))
         (vp (viewport))
         (offset-x (- (rect :x) (vp :x)))
         (offset-y (- (rect :y) (vp :y))))
    (if (or (< offset-x 0) (> offset-x (vp :w)))
      (camera-pan-to (+ (vp :x) (rect :x)) (vp :y))
      (if (or (< offset-y 0) (> offset-y (vp :h)))
        (camera-pan-to (vp :x) (+ (vp :y) (rect :y)))))))
```

```motoi
      (let* ((my-pos (entity/pos id))
             (n-pos (entity/pos nid))
             (dx (- (car n-pos) (car my-pos)))
             (dy (- (cadr n-pos) (cadr my-pos))))
        (if (> (+ (* dx dx) (* dy dy)) 0.1)
```

```motoi
(define (run-state-machine entity input)
  (let ((st (entity/state entity)))
    (cond ((equal? st 'idle)
           (if (> input 0) (entity/goto! entity 'running)))
          ((equal? st 'running)
           (if (= input 0) (entity/goto! entity 'idle))
           (if (< input 0) (entity/goto! entity 'backing)))
          ((equal? st 'backing)
           (if (= input 0) (entity/goto! entity 'idle))))))
(let ((char (world/spawn 'char 50 50)))
  (entity/goto! char 'idle)
  (run-state-machine char 1)
  (run-state-machine char 0)
  (run-state-machine char -1))
```

```motoi
             (entity/goto! id 'chase)

          ((eq? s 'attack)
           (entity/sprite! id 'enemy-attack)
           (on-tick 10 (lambda (_)
             (entity/goto! id 'chase)))))))
```

```motoi
(define (nearest-entity pos radius)
  (let ((all-entities (world/find (lambda (e) #t))))
    (fold-left (lambda (best e)
                 (let ((d (geom/distance (car pos) (cdr pos)
                                         (entity/x e) (entity/y e))))
                   (if (< d radius)
                       (if (or (null? best) (< d (car best)))
                           (cons d e) best)
                       best)))
               null
               all-entities)))
```

```motoi
(define (take xs n)
  (let loop ((xs xs) (n n) (acc '()))
    (if (or (zero? n) (null? xs))
        (reverse acc)
        (loop (cdr xs) (- n 1) (cons (car xs) acc)))))
(define (adaptive-threshold readings window-size)
  (let loop ((readings readings)
            (window (list))
            (thresholds (list)))
    (if (null? readings)
      (math/avg thresholds)
      (let* ((new-window (cons (car readings) (if (>= (length window) window-size)
                                                   (take window (- window-size 1))
                                                   window)))
             (threshold (math/avg new-window)))
        (loop (cdr readings) new-window (cons threshold thresholds))))))
```

```motoi
    (cond ((null? xs) (reverse acc))
          ((pred (car xs)) (loop (cdr xs) (cons (car xs) acc)))
          (else (loop (cdr xs) acc)))))
(define (order-numbers lst)
  (if (null? (cdr lst))
```

```motoi
  (let loop ((xs xs) (acc '()))

          ((pred (car xs)) (loop (cdr xs) (cons (car xs) acc)))
          (else (loop (cdr xs) acc)))))
```

```motoi
(define (play-progression changes)
  (for-each (lambda (chord-data)
    (let ((section (car chord-data))
          (pitches (cadr chord-data))
          (dur (caddr chord-data)))
      (synth/chord section pitches dur)))
    changes))
(play-progression '((strings (C4 E4 G4) 2.0)

```

Play a sequence of chords; each waits or overlaps depending on the timeline.
Give me an expert example of `synth/chord`.

```motoi
(define (orchestrate-motif root chord-type sections durations)
  (define (pitch-offset base semitones) base) ;; simplified pitch calc
  (define (build-chord root type)
    (cond
      ((eq? type 'maj) (list root (pitch-offset root 4) (pitch-offset root 7)))
      ((eq? type 'min) (list root (pitch-offset root 3) (pitch-offset root 7)))
      ((eq? type 'dim) (list root (pitch-offset root 3) (pitch-offset root 6)))))
  (let loop ((secs sections) (durs durations))
    (when (and (pair? secs) (pair? durs))
      (synth/chord (car secs) (build-chord root chord-type) (car durs))
      (loop (cdr secs) (cdr durs)))))
(orchestrate-motif 'C4 'maj '(strings brass woodwinds) '(2.0 1.5 1.0))
```

Compose a motif across orchestral sections; chord shape is derived from root and type, then played on each section with a specific duration.

```motoi
      (cond ((and (eq? (entity/kind id-a) 'player)
                  (eq? (entity/kind id-b) 'coin))
             (entity/despawn! id-b)
             (act 'sound 'coin-collect))
            ((and (eq? (entity/kind id-a) 'enemy)
                  (eq? (entity/kind id-b) 'enemy))
             (world/impulse! id-a -1 0)
             (world/impulse! id-b 1 0)))))
    (world/collisions))
  (next 'resolve-collisions ctx))
```

Multi-branch collision dispatcher in a state function; rebound enemies, collect coins.

```motoi
(define (solve-constraint-once a b rest)
  (let* ((pa (entity/pos a))
         (pb (entity/pos b))
         (gap (- (entity/distance a b) rest))
         (dir-x (/ (- (car pb) (car pa)) (+ (entity/distance a b) 0.001)))
         (dir-y (/ (- (cadr pb) (cadr pa)) (+ (entity/distance a b) 0.001))))

    (world/impulse! b (* gap -0.5 dir-x) (* gap -0.5 dir-y))))
```

```motoi
    (if (null? kinds)
      best
      (let* ((cand (world/nearest npc-id (car kinds)))
             (dist (if (eq? cand 'nan) 999999 (entity/distance npc-id cand))))
```
